former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} ({{ill|lt=801|Q30008|WD=Q30008|1=801}}-{{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}}) | 本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= Summer festival (July 30, 31)
Autumn festival (October 20, 21) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yahagi Shrine''' (やはぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Minamihonmachi, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called "'''Betsugū Hachiman'''," and "Yahagi" is also read as "Yatsukuri." === Overview === This area is said to have originally been the residence site of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahagi no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}}, a collateral branch of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and the shrine is their tutelary shrine. In the 11th century it became a Kamo-betsugū (Yao Kamo-betsugū) of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman-gū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, and in the Edo period it performed Kamo service duties for Iwashimizu Hachiman-gū. This is why it is called Betsugū Hachiman. Several large {{ill|lt=camphor trees|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} grow in the precincts. In addition, a large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|lt=ichō|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree in front of the shrine has many breast-like protrusions on its trunk, and there is a folk belief that decocting and drinking this bark increases breast milk. The shrine preserves a "{{ill|lt=sankakubuchi shinju-kyō|Q11357232|WD=Q11357232|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}" mirror said to have been excavated from the precincts, designated a Yao City tangible cultural property. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity :* {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} : ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct === File:Yahagi-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|Honden File:Yahagi-jinja (Yao), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yahagi-shrine, Yao06.JPG|Great ginkgo (a branch broke in 2017) File:Yahagi-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === * Kotohira Shrine - Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto * Hakusan Shrine - Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto * Yasaka Shrine - Susanoo-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - Inari Ōkami File:Yahagi-jinja (Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Kotohira Shrine, Hakusan Shrine, Yasaka Shrine File:Yahagi-jinja (Yao), Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Festivals === *Summer festival - July 30, 31 *Autumn festival - October 20, 21 === Cultural properties === === Yao City designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Sankakubuchi Shinju-kyō mirror - privately owned, kept at Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts. Designated March 31, 1993 (Heisei 5)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website).. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Yahagi shrine, Yao}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www3.ocn.ne.jp/~tohara/naka-yahagi.html |title=Yahagi Shrine (Yao) |date=20110712223411}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yahagi Shrine, Yao]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yahagi Shrine, Yao]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yahagi Shrine, Yao]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]]
